title: Switch 2 commute days to public transit
description: Taking the bus or train just twice a week instead of driving cuts your commute emissions by 40%. It's the single highest-impact transport swap.

title: Replace one short-haul flight with train
description: A 500km train journey emits ~90% less CO₂ than the same flight. For European travel, Eurostar and Railjet are often comparable in time.

title: Cycle or walk trips under 3km
description: Short car trips under 3km emit disproportionately more per km due to cold engine starts. Cycling these also saves parking costs.

title: Join a carpool for your commute
description: Sharing your commute with one other person halves your per-person transport emissions immediately, with zero lifestyle change.

title: Work from home one extra day per week
description: Each WFH day eliminates a full commute round-trip. Over a year, even one extra day saves hundreds of kg CO₂.

title: Go plant-based 2 days per week
description: Meat-free Monday (and Wednesday!) reduces your diet footprint by ~15%. Swapping beef for legumes is the single biggest diet lever.

title: Halve your beef consumption
description: Beef produces 20x more emissions than chicken and 50x more than lentils per gram of protein. Even small reductions have huge impact.

title: Plan meals to cut food waste by half
description: The average person wastes 30% of food they buy. Meal planning apps, proper storage, and a weekly 'use-it-up' dinner makes a big difference.

title: Buy local and seasonal produce
description: Seasonal local food has dramatically lower transport and refrigeration footprint. Visit a farmers' market or join a local veg box scheme.

title: Switch to a renewable energy tariff
description: Switching to a 100% renewable electricity supplier can eliminate up to 1.5 tonnes of CO₂e per year with a single phone call.

title: Lower thermostat by 1°C in winter
description: Each degree reduction saves ~8% on heating bills and emissions. A smart thermostat (e.g., Nest) can automate this for you.

title: Replace remaining bulbs with LED
description: LED bulbs use 75% less energy and last 25x longer than incandescents. Replacing 10 bulbs typically saves ~$100/year.

title: Reduce shower time by 2 minutes
description: A 2-minute shorter shower saves ~40 litres of water and the energy to heat it — adding up to ~10 kg CO₂e/month.

title: Unplug devices on standby
description: Standby power ('vampire power') accounts for 5-10% of home electricity use. Smart power strips automate this effortlessly.

title: Buy secondhand clothes and electronics
description: Manufacturing new clothes accounts for 10% of global carbon emissions. Thrift stores, Vinted, and eBay reduce this to near zero.

title: Use your phone for one extra year
description: 80% of a smartphone's lifetime emissions occur during manufacturing. Keeping it one extra year reduces its annual footprint by 40%.

title: Consolidate online orders (fewer deliveries)
description: Each home delivery package has a carbon cost. Consolidating to 1-2 orders per week vs. daily deliveries cuts shipping emissions significantly.

title: Repair items instead of replacing
description: Repair cafés, YouTube tutorials, and local repair shops can extend the life of appliances, clothes, and electronics by years.
